review_text: No tiene habitaciones, solo ofrece el espacio para acampar o poner tu caravan o trailer, un enchufe y llave de agua, hay baños con ducha, espacio para cocinar, lavar loza y lavadoras de ropa (esta ultima con un valor adicional)

The camping site was nice and amenities were sufficient and kept well maintained and clean.
The staff are great and Dell especially is lovely.

Every year we stay at the Holiday Park for the Easter Holidays and attend the Shepparton Easter Lawn Tennis Competition. The caretakers really look after the park and a credit to their commitment. The highlight is the close proximity to the Goulburn River (and walking/bike trails) the Victoria Lake and council park amenities there. Things like a skate park, zip line, and an Aquatic centre. We stayed in our camper van on a grassy powered site among the trees. Nice and peaceful.

The Good:
I want to start by saying the staff here are fantastic—very friendly and they actually warned me about the track noise upon check-in, which I appreciated. The park itself is well-maintained and in a very central location for exploring Shepparton. The lake is beautiful and clearly well-managed as a bird sanctuary; it’s a lovely view to wake up to.

​The Reality of Waterfront Sites:
We booked a lakefront site, but "lakefront" here actually means "walking-track front." This has to be the busiest walking track in the Southern Hemisphere. From 6:00 AM until late at night, there is a constant stream of people doing laps and having loud conversations right outside your door.
​More concerning is the "high-speed traffic." E-bikes and e-scooters fly past at what feels like 50+ km/h. If you have a toddler, this is a major safety concern as they are mere meters from the track. We also dealt with constant noise from local dirtbikes rev-bombing and limiter-bashing along the track and nearby streets throughout the evening.

​The Breaking Point:
The biggest issue, however, was the noise from the adjacent train line. I knew the line was there, but at 1:00 AM last night, V/Line began track works, using a crane to drop concrete sleepers onto the ballast just 30m from our camper. The sound of tons of concrete hitting rocks for two hours straight was deafening. It kept our toddler awake and screaming from 1:00 AM to 3:00 AM, completely ruining our night.

​Verdict:
I understand the park has no control over the public walking track or V/Line maintenance schedules, but these factors combined to make our stay incredibly stressful. If you do stay here:
​Avoid the waterfront sites if you value privacy or have small kids.
​Bring heavy-duty earplugs.
​Be prepared for a very "urban" camping experience.
​Unfortunately, despite the park's best efforts, the external noise means we won't be returning to Shepparton or this park again.

Booked in over the phone to stay over Easter and nothing was a problem needed storage for van for couple of days that was not a problem either park situated close to town by a lake with plenty of walking trails also next door to information centre will definitely come back
Well maintained caravan park set in a beautiful location.
Great country style van park. Close to everything. Great walks around the lake. Friendly staff throughout the park.
Well maintained park in great location and good value for money.

We had a power site was close to the amenities. The park is very well maintained and clean. The staff are incredible so friendly and helpful. The cost to stay was less than most. Definitely worth a visit

Couldn't fault this experience.  From friendly polite check in, to well kept grounds and amenities. And pet friendly too. Staff coming around saying hello and checking all is good. |Would recommend this caravan park to anyone.

Great quiet park, close to shops, parks and best of all walking/bike tracks both directions.
Kids playground and pool are on the other side of the lake, with easy access via tracks

Great park |Clean tidy amenities and environment throughout the park. Clean convenient laundry facilities |Close to town and shops and walking and  bike trail to town |Quite even though close to rail line |Very helpful friendly staff gave us great tips on where to go and what attractions around town |Value for money

The park is in a great location close to the lake and the wonderful SAM building in Shepparton. Sites are grassy and well maintained. The amenities are excellent and very clean. Not a big camp kitchen and it shares the room with the laundry.|Staff are very friendly. Security is very good and welcomed as the camp is close to public walking areas and roads. A guy drove around regularly at night and they have cameras to pick up any risks.|Its close to a rail track but that was not an issue really.|All up I would strongly recommend.
Lovely caravan park. Large sites and well  maintained grounds and amenities. One of the most peaceful stays we’ve had. Close to shops and sporting events.

We have been here 3 times now, we love the how green the park is. The staff are amazing and ever so helpful. It’s close to aqua moves, exhibitions and you can walk into town. There are lots of lovely walks close to the caravan park. The town is well equipped with shopping and dining.

This was easily the worst camping experience I’ve ever had. Security is non-existent; strangers are constantly walking past your tent, and the proximity to the bathrooms means dealing with noise all night long. The kitchen facilities are tiny, and food is frequently stolen from the shared refrigerator. To make matters worse, the manager is incredibly rude and becomes defensive if you voice any concerns. There are also permanent residents who are quite aggressive; they’ll yell at you or try to kick you out just for using the kitchen. I strongly advise everyone to stay away from this place
Lovely place to stay

Beautiful natural caravan park set amongst tall gum trees that are a host to flocks of cockatoos. Duck families waddling around the park from the lake. Very peaceful but close to everything. The amenities were spotlessly clean and staff very friendly and helpful
Definitely a lack of toilets and showers, obviously not a concern if you are self contained. Very nice surroundings and walking distance to the CBD
Loved our stay here. Right on the lake and walking path goes right past.
Great park, very clean and well maintained. Close to shopping. Easy access to sites. Definitely recommend.

I wouldn't know.

No one answered our calls, even in the middle of the day. Got the message that office only opened 9-5 yet no one answered the phone all afternoon.  We couldn't book online because we need question answered.  Exactly the same with other parks we tried to phone. No answer, no return call.

Eventually we found another smaller park who actually answered the phone and gave us a beaut spot for the night.

Definitely won't go near any of the big parks here again.  Stick with the small family owned.
Great location, large powered sites clean amenities.

Beautifully kept park with clean, ample amenities, friendly staff, in an incredible location. Picturesque and quiet. Perfect stay for the Shepparton running festival and the Art Museum. Will absolutely be back!
Most excellent holiday park, right by the lake, great for a walk or run, the Bird life is fantastic (Pelican, Swans, small aquatic birds) No cabins but you can have lakeside sites.

No refund for honest mistake – offered useless credit

Accidentally booked the wrong caravan park and contacted them immediately to explain. They refused a refund and only offered a credit, which is no use to me as I won’t be returning.

I understand having policies, but there was time to rebook the site and they showed zero flexibility or understanding. Poor customer service — won’t be booking here again.

For this so called family friendly holiday park my husband got bitten by a pitbull dog that is staying at the park. We informed the management and they told us that the dog will staying put. My husband had to get to stitched at emergency care as well a tetanus shot. We since heard there have been other reports about the same dog and owners.
Nice campground. Thank you for the hospitality

Rude staff. The permanent residents steal your food and act like they own the place. They don’t like sharing the camp kitchen with other guests, and when you’re trying to cook, they’ll come over to insult, yell at, and harass you, claiming you spend too much time in the kitchen (even though that’s impossible since you work from 7 a.m. to 5 p.m., Monday to Saturday). On Sundays, you’ll have to eat out just to avoid being mistreated by Chris (hopefully you don’t meet him). They even installed cameras to monitor how long you take to cook.

Don’t bother complaining—permanent residents and the caravan park managers are all family, which means the staff will also gang up on you and insult you. The place is unsafe, completely open with no separation from the lake park, and the camping area is right next to a 24-hour public bathroom, so get ready to hear drunk fights all night long.

I deeply regret choosing this place to stay. I’ve honestly had a horrible few days here.

Looking for an unforgettable stay? This place truly delivers—just maybe not in the way you'd hope.
If you've ever dreamed of a camping experience with zero security, open access to anyone passing by, and a few permanent residents who might just help themselves to your food or belongings, you're in luck. Leaving things in the fridge? A bold move.

The camp kitchen offers a unique social experience—especially when you're greeted with insults instead of "hello." As for the staff, they're consistently unpleasant, with the exception of a rare kind soul. Special mention to the maintenance man: often drunk, verbally abusive, and occasionally seen mistreating his dog. He treats guests with the same warmth you'd expect from someone who's had a long, bad day… every day.

Electrical outlets? Oh, they’re a real thrill. Some are damaged, others half-exposed, all of them a delightful safety hazard. And just to keep the excitement alive, there are no warning signs or indicators whatsoever—because who wouldn’t want a little surprise with their morning coffee?

In short, if self-respect and safety are on your checklist, maybe keep driving. You've been warned.

SCAM FALSE ADVERTISING THERE IS NO CABINS LIKE THEY SHOW ...
It is a thief's paradise the public walking track runs through and no fences even from railway line we were told no trouble in last 6mths haha Bullshit lies after a few days the local permanents said many things stolen in last 3 weeks. We were told they have a security drive around HAHAHA its a permanent caravan guy Chris who drives at different times but the thieves see the car driving and lay low. We had everything hidden we didn't ride our bikes around had them chained in annex 5 of them they cut annex open layer bikes down together and stole wheels that were not chained. Very suspicious park the cameras are not manned no one cares what happens .
Even cars drive thru somehow.
I RECOMMEND DO NOT STAY THERE GO ELSEWHERE OR ANOTHER TOWN.
We cut our stay short didn't sleep could not leave our van .
Never returning to Shepparton.

Luckily enough to have a GTHO falcon camp next to us.
The GT club was having a show in town on the weekend.
Great caravan park. Good sites. Staff friendly

We camped here in a group of three, caravan sites on the river side were very spacious, staff were very welcoming and friendly, amenities are good, not far from the Art ?Museum and Sams Cafe. Bike track goes through park, good track, lots of parrots and ducks. We had a great time staying here and exploring Shepparton staying in this location only 8 mins or so driving from the main attractions.
Fabulous park with huge sites amazing amenities very friendly helpful staff and loads of bird life. White cockatoos in their hundreds noisy but entertaining 😆
Great place to stay whilst visiting Shepparton

Unfortunately we didn't end up staying the night. I'm sure if you have a caravan or book a cabin it is fine, but be wary if you book a camp site. The park is openly accessible to the public with a 24 hour public shower and toilet very close to the sites. As soon as we arrived a gentleman approached us and started asking us question about our work life and how long we were staying at the park. He was not staying at the facility and we very quickly felt unsafe realising that he could come back at any time and open our tent while we were sleeping or go through our things. I did want to give him the benefit of the doubt but the anxiety convinced us that it was better to be safe than sorry so we left and went to another campsite. I feel the site could have made it clearer just HOW accessible the area is to the public. As the cost for 3 nights was $140+ for an unpowered site, I feel a fence at the very least could be put in place to keep camp goers safe. Further, the bathrooms were not clean enough to justify that price either. Really disappointed.
Best caravan park in shepparton. Staff great, the site we have is large green at the moment and close to all amenities. About 700 mtrs to aldi via a very pleasant walk around the lake

Located near the Victoria Lake Aquatic centre and a few metres from the Shepparton information centre, this park is ideally located and with a 10-minute easy walk along wheelchair ♿️ friendly paths to the shopping centre where you can find Aldi, Dan Murphy, Coles, the Shepparton Club and numerous other shops and restaurants. The caravan park doesn't have the bells and whistles other parks have with such things as kids play equipment and games room, but it does have a friendly relaxed atmosphere with lots of bird life such as swans, water fowl, pelicans, ducks, magpies,  corellas, cockatoos +++. Bring your camera, wine, and a chair to relax.
Receptionist Staff rude and not friendly at all.
Caravan Park is very big, caravan spots are very spacious and grounds are well kept.
We lived in the tent here for 9 weeks and it was very welcoming.

What a quiet and beautiful park
Great place to put a van for a night
Best location in Shepparton, clean facilities, great walk around the lake. Considering it's in town very little noise.

Stayed over grand finial.
Site was amazing. Walking distance to fantastic kids parks and free art museum.
Bathrooms clean.
Donna was just so lovely.
Massive bonus was the grand final party with bbq
Kids had a ball.
Wonderful park with very clean amenities
Nice park with good access to lake and bike/walking trails. Staff super helpful and park and amenities clean and tidy. Very happy with our short stay.

What a lovely caravan park.
So well looked after... beautifully manicured!
Very clean amenities.
Great staff!!!!!! Very friendly!
Just behind the c/p is a lovely river, and alongside the park is a fantastic lake. There are cement walking paths all the way around the lake.
Kids playgrounds, exercising equipment, skate park, a big building,  not  sure what it was, maybe a club ? With pools.
Dog friendly.
Lots of people walking and exercising!
This caravan park is definitely worth $36 per night for a powered site.
Very comfortable park to stay in 😉

We had our own camper
Showers and toilets were warm and clean well looked after
Staff was very helpful
Laundry was easy to use
Close to the tourist centre,  museum and the park, so we had some nice walks close to the park.
We stayed for 4 nights, Tyson was very helpful and friendly. Perfect location for daily walk around the lake. We will definitely be back, thank you for a wonderful stay.
Nice place to stay managers are polite and friendly, walking distance  to town  will definitely stay again

Nice staff.
Great Location
Grassed sites

Ok park for camping but two things that they cannot control. The freight train rumbles through adjacent in the dead of night. And you take your life in your hands if trying to turn right at the end of the street onto the highway. Better to turn left and do a U turn

Did not feel safe here at all. Location to the walking track and town was excellent, but the fact that the park was not fenced, we felt unsafe. People were cutting through the park from the walking track to housing on the other side at all hours. We were told to lock everything up and understand why. Reading a review after we checked in, someone had their tyres slashed. The park itself is undergoing site improvements and the grassed areas were well maintained.  Most sites were under gum trees, which are known to drop limbs without notice.
Great location to the Lake and City Centre
Great sites for caravans  clean amenities  great location  walking distance to town  friendly staff
Beautiful place
Great park.

Its back. The floods are over and this caravan park is great.
This is a council run Caravan Park but the managers running it are super keen on looking after their customers. Donna was most friendly and helpful with a site location. They seem to be out there mowing the grass, trimming, watering the grass. If you want a grassy site you are in luck as they are all grassy. They keep the grass like carpet. Great Camp kitchen combined with the laundry. Works well. You will not be disappointed being so close to Victoria Lake.

So much better than when we were here about two years ago and the biggest difference is Tyson. He has the park looking fabulous and is without doubt the best host we have met in all our travels around Australia.
Loved this place, can't fault this place.  Would highly recommend

Lovely Park. Facilities cleaned twice daily and spotless. Great location- on the lake and short walk to town. Tyson was a great host and very helpful. The staff put on an awesome Christmas dinner by the river - had a great time and shows they really care about their guests. Highly recommend!
Very helpful staff. Great park good site amenities good and clean great walks around lake

Anyone who gives this holiday park a negative review don't realise how far this park has come since the devastating floods in October, 2022.
The park was absolutely demolished and it has only just reopened in June, 2023. The tireless work and effort from Tyson has brought the park back to a lovely outback atmosphere with brand new toilet blocks and laundry facilities. The camp kitchen has everything you need to cook up a great meal. All the sites have nice green grass which is constantly being maintained by Tyson with watering, mowing and trimming. We absolutely love it here we have been here for a month and staying a couple more. Pricing is very reasonable. Please come and support the park and get to meet the very helpful and easy going Tyson, you won't be disappointed.

What an awesome Christmas dinner put on for park patrons by the park manager Tyson and his mum. Plenty of food, plenty of laughs and new friendships made in a picturesque setting. Well done Tyson you made Christmas night very special for those who were travelling and away from family.

No fly screens on the windows of the amenities, insects and bugs everywhere. The showers are not cleaned regularly - build up of grime on every corner/ wall of the shower cubicles, floor not clean and most of the time the door doesn't lock! Key pad doesn't work.  Laundry floor not clean, build up of grime and who knows what on the inside of the dryer and washing machine doors.  One dryer not working.  Everything run down, clothes line falling apart.  Bird droppings and tree debris everywhere.  The one BBQ doesn't work. Neighbours able to play loud music well into the night. Wi Fi was terrible. For the price it was a complete rip off.

Hard place to find...had an old address, should have double checked. Staff was great getting us to our spot once there. Amenities upgraded and looked new,  but, no cleaning of occurred.  Shower floors dirty, no soap in any of the dispensers. Hand air dryer worked OK.  Park is next to a railway line and some trains can be noisy.  Worst noise came from the corellas. ..so many and so noisy. First facility building had one toilet and 2 showers; second building had 4 each. Looked new but in need of cleaning. OK in a pinch.
This is a great CP near the lake, river and town. Stayed on a large concrete based site. Railway line near by and had one come thru at 5.30am just before the Cockatoos started otherwise no probs.
Fantastic spacious park with great walking paths and close to shops.
Close to Lake, information centre and walking tracks.
Camp site in a nice park close to town.  Cleaning of the facilities needs to be more frequent due to the abundance of insects.
This caravan park is absolutely amazing. Sarah was exceptional to deal with and I will definitely be visiting again.
Beautiful spot. Very quiet and peaceful. A little bit too far away from town to be able to walk to anything.
Dedicated staff bringing the Park back to pre flood excellence

Absolutely fantastic recovery job for this c/van park, 1 yr on from the floods. We booked for 1 nite and stayed for 2. Beautiful tranquil setting, large sites,with plenty of room between vans. The river was up again, so river walk was shut off again but it looked good. Tyson on reception was just easy going and lovely to deal with.
Highly recommend this c/van park

Victoria lake holiday park in Shepparton is located next to Victoria lake and the Goulburn river. It is about 2km south of the centre of town. There are a range of sites, including grassy and concrete powered options. The amenities were modern and clean. The check in was easy and the reception was welcoming. It is also close to the start of the Shepparton parkrun. There was some traffic noise overnight from the nearby roads.

Great park. Friendly and efficient staff. Wonderful location near Victoria Lake and Goulburn River for walks and bike riding. Close to spc. Beautiful setting amongst well maintained grassy or slab sites and shady trees. Facilities were great. Will be back again. Thank you for a lovely park.

Great Park to stay at, you feel so remote but so close to town!
Facilities are clean and Park it’s self is so neat and tidy. Will be staying here again.
New management are so friendly and easy to deal with

We were lucky enough to stay at this phenomenal park, Sarah and Tyson are fantastic host's.
We had such a pleasant stay.
Highly recommended!

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Victoria Lake Caravan Park in Shepparton, and I can't say enough good things about this place!

From the moment I arrived, I was greeted by friendly and accommodating staff who made the check-in process a breeze. The park itself is beautifully maintained, with lush greenery and well-kept grounds that create a serene and welcoming atmosphere.

The amenities here are top-notch. The clean and modern facilities, including spotless restrooms and showers, exceeded my expectations for a caravan park. The laundry facilities were also a convenient bonus during my stay.

But what truly sets Victoria Lake Caravan Park apart is its location. Situated right by the picturesque Victoria Lake, the views are breathtaking, especially at sunrise and sunset. It's the perfect place to unwind and relax, whether you're taking a leisurely stroll by the lake, enjoying a picnic, or simply soaking in the natural beauty.

The park's proximity to Shepparton's attractions and amenities is another huge plus. You're just a short drive away from restaurants, shops, and local attractions, making it a convenient base for exploring the area.

I also appreciated the sense of community here. The fellow campers I met during my stay were friendly and welcoming, adding to the overall positive experience.

In conclusion, I  recommend Victoria Lake Caravan Park to anyone looking for a peaceful and well-maintained getaway in Shepparton. Whether you're a seasoned traveler or a first-time camper, this park will exceed your expectations. I'm already looking forward to my next visit!

Great place to stay, lovely large grassy sites. Very well maintained.
New owners are very friendly, amenities beautiful and clean. Highly recommend staying here
Awesome service! Super nice people and great facilities
Great location great price caretaker top bloke.
